title: Build a semantic blog post
description: Replace the generic divs with semantic HTML so the page has a header, navigation, an article, and a footer.

starter.index.html:
My blog
Home About

Hello world

First post.

© 2026

title: Make a login form accessible
description: Associate every input with a <label>, mark required fields with the `required` attribute, and use the right input types (email, password). The form should be keyboard-navigable.

starter.styles.css:
body { font-family: system-ui; padding: 16px; }
form { display: grid; gap: 8px; max-width: 300px; }

title: Build a semantic data table
description: Render a small table of users with proper <thead>, <tbody>, <th scope=col> and a <caption>.

title: Center a box with Flexbox
description: Center the .box element horizontally and vertically inside .container using only flexbox properties.

title: Auto-fit card grid
description: Use CSS Grid with repeat(auto-fit, minmax()) so cards fill the row and wrap to new rows responsively. Minimum card width: 180px.

starter.index.html:
A
B
C
D
E

title: Style button states
description: Style the button so it has visible :hover, :focus-visible and :disabled states with smooth transitions.

title: Sum the elements of an array
description: Implement `sum(arr)` that returns the sum of all numbers in the array. Empty arrays should return 0.

title: Implement debounce
description: Implement `debounce(fn, ms)` that returns a function which only invokes `fn` after `ms` milliseconds have passed since the last call. Calls before the timeout fires must reset it.

tests.name: calls fn after delay
tests.body: let calls = 0; const f = debounce(() => calls++, 20); f(); await wait(50); expect(calls).toBe(1);

tests.name: rapid calls collapse to one
tests.body: let calls = 0; const f = debounce(() => calls++, 20); f(); f(); f(); await wait(50); expect(calls).toBe(1);

tests.name: passes args
tests.body: let last; const f = debounce((x) => (last = x), 10); f(7); await wait(30); expect(last).toBe(7);

title: Deduplicate an array
description: Implement `unique(arr)` that returns a new array with duplicates removed, preserving the original order.

title: Deep-clone a plain object
description: Implement `deepClone(obj)` that recursively clones plain objects and arrays. You may assume only primitives, plain objects and arrays.

tests.name: clones nested object
tests.body: const a = { x: { y: 1 } }; const b = deepClone(a); b.x.y = 9; expect(a.x.y).toBe(1);

tests.name: clones arrays
tests.body: const a = [1, [2, 3]]; const b = deepClone(a); b[1][0] = 99; expect(a[1][0]).toBe(2);

title: Implement groupBy
description: Implement `groupBy(arr, keyFn)` returning an object where keys are produced by keyFn(item) and values are arrays of matching items.

title: Refactor a counter to a custom hook
description: Given a Counter component that uses useState, extract the logic into a custom hook `useCounter(initial)` that returns `{ value, increment, decrement, reset }`.
rubric: Solution should: (1) define `useCounter(initial = 0)` returning { value, increment, decrement, reset }; (2) Counter consumes the hook; (3) functions are stable (useCallback or defined in module/closure); (4) no unnecessary re-renders.

title: Convert to a controlled form
description: The form below uses uncontrolled inputs. Convert it to a controlled form with a single state object and submit handler that logs the values.
rubric: Solution should: (1) hold values in a single useState object, (2) bind value + onChange on each input, (3) prevent default on submit, (4) log the typed values.

title: Add cleanup to an effect
description: The component sets up an interval but never cleans it up. Fix the effect so the interval is cleared on unmount and when the delay prop changes.
rubric: Solution should: (1) capture the interval id, (2) return a cleanup function that calls clearInterval, (3) include delay in deps, (4) avoid the leak when delay changes.

title: Add a Server Action to a form
description: Wire a Server Action `addTodo(formData)` into the given <form>. The action must validate input with Zod and revalidate the page.
rubric: Solution should: (1) define an async function with 'use server', (2) use FormData.get and validate with Zod, (3) call revalidatePath after the mutation, (4) handle the validation error path.

title: Stream a slow data section
description: Wrap the slow <Posts /> component in <Suspense> so the rest of the page renders immediately while posts load. Provide a fallback skeleton.
rubric: Solution should: (1) import Suspense from react, (2) wrap <Posts /> with <Suspense fallback={<Skeleton />}>, (3) keep the rest of the page outside the boundary, (4) explain why this enables streaming.

title: Type-only: PickByValue
description: Define a type `PickByValue<T, V>` that picks the keys of T whose value type extends V. Then assert with sample types.
solution: type PickByValue<T, V> = { [K in keyof T as T[K] extends V ? K : never]: T[K] };
rubric: Use mapped types with key remapping (`as ... extends ... ? K : never`).

title: Model a Result type
description: Define a discriminated union `Result<T, E>` representing success or failure, and a helper `unwrap(r)` that throws on error and returns the value otherwise. Give a usage example.

starter.solution.ts:
// type Result<T, E> = ...
// function unwrap<T, E>(r: Result<T, E>): T { /* TODO */ }

rubric: Solution should: (1) define Result<T,E> = { ok: true; value: T } | { ok: false; error: E }, (2) implement unwrap that narrows on r.ok, (3) show a usage example.

title: Validate API input with Zod
description: Given an unknown JSON body, define a Zod schema for { email: string; age: number } and return either a typed object or a validation error.
rubric: Solution should: (1) define a Zod schema with email validation and age >= 0, (2) call schema.safeParse, (3) return a discriminated result (success with data, failure with error.flatten()).

title: Read a JSON file with fs/promises
description: Write an async function `readUsers(path)` that reads a JSON file, parses it and returns the array of users. Errors should bubble.
rubric: Solution should: (1) await readFile with utf8 encoding, (2) JSON.parse the result, (3) optionally validate the shape, (4) let unexpected errors propagate (no swallowing).

title: Minimal HTTP server
description: Implement a Node http server that responds with JSON `{ ok: true }` on GET / and 404 on anything else.
rubric: Solution should: (1) check req.method === 'GET' and req.url === '/', (2) set Content-Type: application/json, (3) end with JSON.stringify({ ok: true }), (4) return 404 for everything else.

title: Pipe a file through gzip
description: Use stream pipeline to gzip an input file into an output file. Errors must reject.
rubric: Solution should: (1) await pipeline(createReadStream(input), createGzip(), createWriteStream(output)), (2) let errors throw via the awaited pipeline, (3) avoid manual .pipe() chaining.
